Patch Set 1:

(3 comments)

This isn't exactly my area of code, but in general terms this looks like it would make the assertion *more* likely to be hit? 

However, the value of the loop is also a bit questionable if more IO can be submitted while this is waiting. Either IO needs to be stopped at a higher level, or the code needs to accept that this can happen (esp. for PFL layouts that always change while IO is happening).

Patch Set 1:

By the way, I'd like to see this *specific* patch continued, just reframed as a code cleanup, because a while loop with the wait condition around wait_event_idle is definitely bad form.  wait_event_idle *is* a while loop on the condition.

But it's not a functional change, just a (useful) clarification.

Patch Set 1:

At this point I like to see if I could reproduce the problem. Any suggest where this race is coming from?

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Looking more at the code I think something is going on with the lov_conf_lock. Sadly I couldn't reproduce this with maloo.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

> At this point I like to see if I could reproduce the problem. Any suggest where this race is coming from?

everything which can increment active ios is serialised by lov_conf_freeze(), except the case of io->ci_type  == CIT_MISC && io->ci_ignore_layout. I suspect something like ll_writepage()/cl_io_init( ... ,CIT_MISC, ...) can race with lov_layout_change().
Uploaded patch set 2.

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-16835 target: server_ioctl() should return ENOTTY

commit.subject: LU-9839 lov: ensure lo_waitq is drains for layout change
commit.message:
LU-9839 lov: ensure lo_waitq is drains for layout change

Current we are running into this assert:

[28412.673882] Lustre: Mounted orion-client
[29077.786517] LustreError: 22078:0:(lov_object.c:1284:lov_layout_change()) ASSERTION( atomic_read(&lov->lo_active_ios) == 0 ) failed:
[29077.800692] LustreError: 22078:0:(lov_object.c:1284:lov_layout_change()) LBUG
[29077.809341] Pid: 22078, comm: globus-gridftp- 3.10.0-1160.80.1.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Sat Oct 8 18:13:21 UTC 2022
[29077.821098] Call Trace:
[29077.824601] [<0>] libcfs_call_trace+0x90/0xf0 [libcfs]
[29077.831107] [<0>] lbug_with_loc+0x4c/0xa0 [libcfs]
[29077.837272] [<0>] lov_conf_set+0xb0e/0xbc0 [lov]
[29077.843300] [<0>] cl_conf_set+0x6b/0x150 [obdclass]
[29077.849543] [<0>] ll_layout_conf+0x81/0x460 [lustre]
[29077.855892] [<0>] ll_layout_refresh+0x6af/0xc10 [lustre]
[29077.862640] [<0>] vvp_io_init+0x357/0x490 [lustre]
[29077.868749] [<0>] cl_io_init0.isra.6+0x93/0x180 [obdclass]
[29077.875662] [<0>] cl_io_init+0x43/0x90 [obdclass]
[29077.881724] [<0>] cl_io_rw_init+0x77/0x210 [obdclass]
[29077.888089] [<0>] ll_file_io_generic+0x2a8/0x1000 [lustre]
[29077.895009] [<0>] ll_file_aio_write+0x5a0/0xa10 [lustre]
[29077.901902] [<0>] ll_file_write+0x119/0x1e0 [lustre]
[29077.908336] [<0>] vfs_write+0xc0/0x1f0
[29077.913302] [<0>] SyS_write+0x55/0xd0

In lov_layout_change() we call old_ops->llo_delete() which in
all cases call lov_layout_wait() to ensure we drain the wait
queue. This should reduce the lo_active_ios to zero before we
encounter the assert. Loking at lov_layout_wait() there is no
need for the while loop. This ensure we always call
wait_event_idle() and wait_event_idle() always waits untils the
condition is meet. Update the assert to report the lo_active_ios
value to ensure we haven't reached saturation.

Test-Parameters: fortestonly trivial testlist=sanity env=ONLY=405,ONLY_REPEAT=100
Change-Id: Ibac44da7f5f3658c0d39084ae4001154271383bc
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
